title: State machine verification: Rust lichen-core
description:
Add compile-time + runtime state machine verification to lichen-core Rust crate.

Rust options:
1. typestate pattern (compile-time, zero-cost)
2. enum + match exhaustiveness (compile-time)
3. runtime transition table (like C, but with debug_assert)

Files: rust/lichen-core/src/*.rs

Prefer typestate where practical (e.g., connection states), runtime for dynamic state (e.g., routing tables).
close_reason: Added compile-time and runtime LOADng route-discovery state verification to rust/lichen-core. Implemented RouteDiscovery<Idle|Searching|Replied|Failed> typestate sequencing for expanding-ring RREQ discovery, matching-RREP acceptance, failure after exhausted rings, and a RouteDiscoveryState runtime transition table with InvalidDiscoveryTransition/InvalidDiscoveryReply errors. Kept scope to rust/lichen-core/src/loadng.rs. Tests/checks: rustfmt lichen-core/src/loadng.rs passed; cargo test -p lichen-core passed with 50/50 tests; cargo clippy -p lichen-core --all-targets -- -D warnings -A clippy::manual_is_multiple_of passed; git diff --check passed. cargo clippy -p lichen-core --all-targets -- -D warnings without the allow is blocked by a pre-existing ipv6.rs manual_is_multiple_of lint tracked as project-LICHEN-1i7v. Review pass 1: found and fixed public/private trait-bound warning by making DiscoveryMarker public; also removed unrelated rustfmt churn in addr.rs/announce.rs/ipv6.rs/udp.rs. Review pass 2: no findings; verified diff is scoped to LOADng state-machine code plus Beads metadata and generated rust/target was removed. Follow-up created: project-LICHEN-1i7v for the out-of-scope clippy lint.
